ocr.verbatim:
NAME : 
AGE : 75YRS
CATH ID : 2397/03/2026
DATE : 24/03/2026
SEX : MALE
IP NO : AHH-1778/03/26
CORONARY ANGIOPLASTY REPORT (PCI)
ROUTE : RIGHT FEMORAL ARTERY
Procedure : PCI TO RCA
Access : 7F SHEATH
RFA/RRA : 130/80 mmhg
Guiding Catheter : 6F XBRCA 3.0
Inj Heparin : 10000 IU
Coronary wire : Fielder Fc Wire
Pre-dilatation : Sapphire1.5 X 10 mm balloon at 16 atm.
Mini Trek 2.0 X 12 mm balloon at 16 atm.
Stent : RCA-GRACE 2.5 X 28 mm at 11 atm.[DES]
GRACE 2.5 X 32 mm at 11 atm.[DES]
Post-dilatation : Sapphire nc 3.0 x 12 mm balloon at 19 atm.
POST PCI ACT : 290
Final result : Excellent final result, no haze / dissection / thrombus
TIMI III Flow
Residual stenosis 0%
RCA : Using 6F XBRCA 3.0 catheter ,Right coronary ostium was engaged and
0.014 Fielder Fc wire was advanced in the distal RCA.The lesion was dilated
with Sapphire1.5 X 10 mm balloon at 16 atm, Mini Trek 2.0 X 12 mm balloon
at 16 atm.and stented with GRACE 2.5 X 28 mm at 11 atm. GRACE 2.5 X 32
mm at 11 atm. Post-dilatation done with Sapphire nc 3.0 x 12 mm balloon at
19 atm. with an excellent final result.
PCI TO RCA WITH 2 STENT DONE
LONG TREM DUAL ANTIPLATELETS.
Note: All stents having inherent risk of restenosis and thrombosis. Please
continue antiplatelet drugs regularly. Do not stop medicines without
Consulting Physician or Cardiologist
MBBS, DNB (Med), DNB (Card)
Senior Interventional Cardiolog
सूचना * पुड़ल वेल्स टेलाना हा कागद सोलबत आणावा. * औषधी अॉलर्जी असत्यास प्रथम सांगणे. * औषधे डॉक्टरांना दाखवून घ्या.

records.summary: This is a typed clinical report for a patient who underwent Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI) to the Right Coronary Artery (RCA). The procedure involved stent placement and balloon angioplasty, resulting in an excellent final outcome with TIMI III flow and 0% residual stenosis.
key_observations: Procedure: PCI to RCA via right femoral artery.
Stents deployed: RCA-GRACE 2.5 X 28 mm and GRACE 2.5 X 32 mm.
Final result: Excellent, with no dissection, thrombus, or haze; TIMI III flow; 0% residual stenosis.
Patient age: 75 years, male.
Date of procedure: 24/03/2026.
Note: Patient must continue dual antiplatelet therapy and consult physician before stopping medications.

records.stage_evidence: Stents and balloons visible in coronary arteries

records.summary: This is a typed coronary angiography report for a 75-year-old male patient, detailing stenosis findings in the left anterior descending and right coronary arteries, with an impression of 'Disease of Vascular Disease' and a recommendation for PTCA (Percutaneous Transluminal Coronary Angioplasty) on LAD and RCA.
key_observations: Patient is 75 years old, male.
Left Anterior Descending artery shows 70% stenosis at ostium.
Right Coronary Artery shows 70% stenosis at ostium and 80% stenosis in mid segment.
Recommendation is PTCA to LAD and RCA.
Report includes a note in Marathi advising to submit the document with the patient and show medicines to the doctor.

records.stage_evidence: Handwritten annotations indicating 'Dvd' (likely stent) and 'IMP: PD to LAD & RCA' (interventional procedure performed)
